Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class

We can create a UdpClient instance and send a packet to the server

We can then measure the time taken to send the packet using the DateTime.Now property

We can then calculate the time difference between the send time and the receive time

This will give us the time it took for the packet to be sent to the server

We can also handle any exceptions that may occur during the process

This method is simple and efficient for this type of task

We can also use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class to measure the time taken

This will give us a more accurate measurement of the time taken

We can also use the System.Net.NetworkInformation.NetworkInterface.GetIsNetworkAvailable() method to check if the network is available before sending the packet

This will prevent the application from sending packets when the network is not available
